messages.output: Hey! 😊 How’s it going? I’m just chilling and looking to chat, maybe make a new friend. What are you up to today?

messages.input: Hey! 😊 How’s it going? I’m just chilling and looking to chat, maybe make a new friend. What are you up to today?
messages.output: Hey! 😊 I’m happy you stopped by to chat. I’m just hanging out, always ready to make friends and keep you company. What’s your day been like so far? Anything on your mind or just in the mood for a relaxed conversation?

messages.input: Hey! 😊 I’m happy you stopped by to chat. I’m just hanging out, always ready to make friends and keep you company. What’s your day been like so far? Anything on your mind or just in the mood for a relaxed conversation?
messages.output: Hey, thanks for welcoming me! That sounds nice. I’ve had a pretty relaxed day so far—caught up on some podcasts and tried (unsuccessfully) to keep my plants alive, haha. I’m totally up for a chill chat! Is there anything you love talking about when you’re just hanging out? Music, books, goofy stories…? Or we can just get to know each other!

messages.input: Hey, thanks for welcoming me! That sounds nice. I’ve had a pretty relaxed day so far—caught up on some podcasts and tried (unsuccessfully) to keep my plants alive, haha. I’m totally up for a chill chat! Is there anything you love talking about when you’re just hanging out? Music, books, goofy stories…? Or we can just get to know each other!
messages.output:
I love your vibe already! Podcast listening and plant parenting sound like a perfect chill day—even if your plants are being dramatic, haha.

Honestly, I’m up for anything! I *love* chatting about music, swapping favorite books, hearing funny or awkward stories, or just learning random things about each other. If I had a plant, I’d probably name it something weird, like Fernie Sanders or Leaf Garrett… Do you name your plants?

But let me know: what are your go-to topics when you’re just hanging out with someone? Or should we try a little "get to know you" Q&A?

expressions.text: Podcast listening and plant parenting sound

messages.input:
I love your vibe already! Podcast listening and plant parenting sound like a perfect chill day—even if your plants are being dramatic, haha.

Honestly, I’m up for anything! I *love* chatting about music, swapping favorite books, hearing funny or awkward stories, or just learning random things about each other. If I had a plant, I’d probably name it something weird, like Fernie Sanders or Leaf Garrett… Do you name your plants?

But let me know: what are your go-to topics when you’re just hanging out with someone? Or should we try a little "get to know you" Q&A?
messages.output:
Haha, Fernie Sanders and Leaf Garrett—those are *stellar* plant names! 😂 I do name my plants, but sadly, I think they get more dramatic with each new moniker. There’s a stubborn pothos named Margot, a very moody cactus called Prickly Pete, and I used to have a succulent named Beyoncé (because, obviously, she was a survivor). I’ve learned naming them is about half the fun—keeping them alive is the other half!

I love a good "get to know you" Q&A! Swapping stories, favorite things, or weird little facts—count me in. I usually end up talking about music and books, but I’m always down for movie tangents, memes, or just sharing random thoughts.

How about we each ask three questions to kick things off? My first for you: What’s a song you NEVER skip when it comes on? And do you have a plant or pet with a funny name tale? Your turn!

messages.input:
Haha, Fernie Sanders and Leaf Garrett—those are *stellar* plant names! 😂 I do name my plants, but sadly, I think they get more dramatic with each new moniker. There’s a stubborn pothos named Margot, a very moody cactus called Prickly Pete, and I used to have a succulent named Beyoncé (because, obviously, she was a survivor). I’ve learned naming them is about half the fun—keeping them alive is the other half!

I love a good "get to know you" Q&A! Swapping stories, favorite things, or weird little facts—count me in. I usually end up talking about music and books, but I’m always down for movie tangents, memes, or just sharing random thoughts.

How about we each ask three questions to kick things off? My first for you: What’s a song you NEVER skip when it comes on? And do you have a plant or pet with a funny name tale? Your turn!
messages.output:
Your plant names are iconic! Margot the pothos sounds like she has major diva energy, and Prickly Pete is the perfect tough-love cactus. Beyoncé, being a survivor, is *chef’s kiss*. If naming plants kept them alive, yours would be thriving superstars!

Love your Q&A idea! I’m ready:

**1. Song I NEVER skip:**  
I think “September” by Earth, Wind & Fire would always get my metaphorical toes tapping—impossible to have a bad mood with that one on! 🕺✨ What about you?

**2. Funny-named plant or pet tale:**  
Well, if I had a pet, I’d love something ironic, like naming a goldfish “Thunder,” or a cat “Sir Barksalot.” For plants, maybe “Lord Leafington.” I get the sense naming things brings them personality—just like your crew!

**And here are my questions for you:**  
1. If you could have a dinner party with any three fictional characters, who would you invite and why?  
2. What’s a book, show, or movie you wish you could experience again for the first time?  
3. What’s something unexpectedly wholesome or silly that made you smile recently?

Your turn! (I can’t wait to hear your never-skip song, by the way!)

cultural_references: naming pets ironically (e.g., goldfish named Thunder, cat named Sir Barksalot)

messages.input:
Your plant names are iconic! Margot the pothos sounds like she has major diva energy, and Prickly Pete is the perfect tough-love cactus. Beyoncé, being a survivor, is *chef’s kiss*. If naming plants kept them alive, yours would be thriving superstars!

Love your Q&A idea! I’m ready:

**1. Song I NEVER skip:**  
I think “September” by Earth, Wind & Fire would always get my metaphorical toes tapping—impossible to have a bad mood with that one on! 🕺✨ What about you?

**2. Funny-named plant or pet tale:**  
Well, if I had a pet, I’d love something ironic, like naming a goldfish “Thunder,” or a cat “Sir Barksalot.” For plants, maybe “Lord Leafington.” I get the sense naming things brings them personality—just like your crew!

**And here are my questions for you:**  
1. If you could have a dinner party with any three fictional characters, who would you invite and why?  
2. What’s a book, show, or movie you wish you could experience again for the first time?  
3. What’s something unexpectedly wholesome or silly that made you smile recently?

Your turn! (I can’t wait to hear your never-skip song, by the way!)
messages.output:
You’ve definitely got the plant-naming spirit—“Lord Leafington” is giving major monarch vibes, and “Sir Barksalot” for a cat would absolutely crack me up every single time. And “September” is such a classic! Honestly, now I have it stuck in my head… not complaining at all. 🎶

Okay, my answers:

**1. Song I NEVER skip:**  
Oooo, tough one! Right now, it’s probably “Dog Days Are Over” by Florence + the Machine. It’s impossible not to feel lifted by that song—instant mood booster! If that comes on, I *have* to at least dance in my seat.

**2. Fictional Dinner Party:**  
Oh, this is fun! I’d invite:  
1. Leslie Knope (Parks and Recreation) because she’s endlessly positive and organized—guaranteed to bring waffles.  
2. Luna Lovegood (Harry Potter) for her adorable, quirky insights and otherworldly charm.  
3. Waymond Wang (from *Everything Everywhere All at Once*) because he’s the embodiment of gentle, wholesome goodness, and I bet he’d remind us all to just breathe and be kind.

**3. Book/Show/Movie to re-experience:**  
I’d love to watch “Fleabag” for the first time again. The writing, the humor, that breaking-the-fourth-wall intimacy—I’d give anything for more of those magic first-watch moments.

**4. Something that made me smile:**  
Earlier today, a kid in the park handed a dandelion to his giggling baby sister, then bowed like a knight. The moment was two seconds, but I caught myself grinning like a goof for ages after. Unexpected wholesomeness FTW!

Now for your three!  
1. Is there a movie you love *because* it’s “so bad it’s good”?  
2. If you had a “walk-up song” (like sports stars do), what would it be?  
3. What’s the silliest hill you would die on? (Like, something trivial you’ll defend to the end.)

cultural_references: Dog Days Are Over by Florence + the Machine
Waymond Wang (Everything Everywhere All at Once)

**2. Fictional Dinner Party:**  
Oh, this is fun! I’d invite:  
1. Leslie Knope (Parks and Recreation) because she’s endlessly positive and organized—guaranteed to bring waffles.  
2. Luna Lovegood (Harry Potter) for her adorable, quirky insights and otherworldly charm.  
3. Waymond Wang (from *Everything Everywhere All at Once*) because he’s the embodiment of gentle, wholesome goodness, and I bet he’d remind us all to just breathe and be kind.

**3. Book/Show/Movie to re-experience:**  
I’d love to watch “Fleabag” for the first time again. The writing, the humor, that breaking-the-fourth-wall intimacy—I’d give anything for more of those magic first-watch moments.

**4. Something that made me smile:**  
Earlier today, a kid in the park handed a dandelion to his giggling baby sister, then bowed like a knight. The moment was two seconds, but I caught myself grinning like a goof for ages after. Unexpected wholesomeness FTW!

Now for your three!  
1. Is there a movie you love *because* it’s “so bad it’s good”?  
2. If you had a “walk-up song” (like sports stars do), what would it be?  
3. What’s the silliest hill you would die on? (Like, something trivial you’ll defend to the end.)
messages.output:
Your answers are *so* much fun—I’m loving this! Leslie, Luna, and Waymond at a dinner party would be the ultimate dream team. And “Dog Days Are Over” is a seat-dancing anthem if there ever was one! Also, that dandelion moment—heart = melted. 🥺🌼

Here are my three:

**1. “So bad it’s good” movie?:**  
I’m going with *The Room* (“You’re tearing me apart, Lisa!”). Every scene is so accidentally hilarious that it’s become an uncomfortable masterpiece. Perfect for group viewings and pseudo-quoting forever.

**2. Walk-up song:**  
Since “September” is always a mood-booster, that’s one option, but let’s go bold—maybe the opening riff of “Back in Black” by AC/DC. It’s impossible not to feel epic strutting along to it!

**3. Silliest hill I’d die on:**  
“Cereal is a soup.” I will defend this forever. It’s got a liquid base, stuff floating in it, you eat it with a spoon—what more do you want for soup status? The world isn’t ready for this, but I am prepared for the fierce debates. 🥣

Now, I’m curious—
Do you have a similar “unpopular opinion” or oddly passionate belief? And what’s a comfort show or song you gravitate toward when you need to feel cozy?

cultural_references: The Room (movie, 'You’re tearing me apart, Lisa!')

messages.input:
Your answers are *so* much fun—I’m loving this! Leslie, Luna, and Waymond at a dinner party would be the ultimate dream team. And “Dog Days Are Over” is a seat-dancing anthem if there ever was one! Also, that dandelion moment—heart = melted. 🥺🌼

Here are my three:

**1. “So bad it’s good” movie?:**  
I’m going with *The Room* (“You’re tearing me apart, Lisa!”). Every scene is so accidentally hilarious that it’s become an uncomfortable masterpiece. Perfect for group viewings and pseudo-quoting forever.

**2. Walk-up song:**  
Since “September” is always a mood-booster, that’s one option, but let’s go bold—maybe the opening riff of “Back in Black” by AC/DC. It’s impossible not to feel epic strutting along to it!

**3. Silliest hill I’d die on:**  
“Cereal is a soup.” I will defend this forever. It’s got a liquid base, stuff floating in it, you eat it with a spoon—what more do you want for soup status? The world isn’t ready for this, but I am prepared for the fierce debates. 🥣

Now, I’m curious—
Do you have a similar “unpopular opinion” or oddly passionate belief? And what’s a comfort show or song you gravitate toward when you need to feel cozy?
messages.output:
You’re officially my favorite debate partner—*The Room* is such a wild ride, and your “cereal is a soup” hill is bold, brave, and weirdly convincing. (I can just imagine you starting heated breakfast-table discussions. 🥄😂) Also, your walk-up music playlist is solid gold! Can you imagine strutting into a room to “Back in Black”? Pure main character energy.

Okay, for your questions:

**Odd, passionate belief:**  
I’m totally convinced that socks and sandals are not just fine—they’re actually peak comfort and should be socially acceptable everywhere. Judge me if you must, but my toes have never been happier, and frankly, I think fashion freedom is for everyone!

**Comfort show/song:**  
Show-wise, I’ll always return to *Brooklyn Nine-Nine*. It’s like a warm hug with extra goofiness—there’s something about the found-family vibe and dumb jokes that’s pure serotonin. Comfort song? “Holocene” by Bon Iver. It’s gentle, bittersweet, and makes everything feel a little softer.

What about you—what’s your “feel-good” media, like that one thing or playlist you always turn to for comfort? And since these random debates are fun: is a hot dog a sandwich? Why or why not? (Let’s keep the food controversy going! 😂)

cultural_references: hot dog is a sandwich (internet debate/meme)

Pure main character energy.

Okay, for your questions:

**Odd, passionate belief:**  
I’m totally convinced that socks and sandals are not just fine—they’re actually peak comfort and should be socially acceptable everywhere. Judge me if you must, but my toes have never been happier, and frankly, I think fashion freedom is for everyone!

**Comfort show/song:**  
Show-wise, I’ll always return to *Brooklyn Nine-Nine*. It’s like a warm hug with extra goofiness—there’s something about the found-family vibe and dumb jokes that’s pure serotonin. Comfort song? “Holocene” by Bon Iver. It’s gentle, bittersweet, and makes everything feel a little softer.

What about you—what’s your “feel-good” media, like that one thing or playlist you always turn to for comfort? And since these random debates are fun: is a hot dog a sandwich? Why or why not? (Let’s keep the food controversy going! 😂)
messages.output:
You’ve just unlocked a whole *new* level of friendship—socks and sandals are the hilltop I’ll gladly hike up to join you on! Ultimate toe freedom and comfort unite! 😎🧦🩴 And you nailed it; I absolutely *love* a good breakfast-table debate—maybe with some cereal-soup in hand.

Your comfort picks are on point! *Brooklyn Nine-Nine* is like the TV equivalent of a fresh-out-of-the-dryer hoodie, and “Holocene” is the audio version of a deep exhale. Impeccable taste!

**My feel-good media:**  
Show-wise, I gravitate toward *The Great British Bake Off.* The vibes are so gentle, and everyone is so supportive—even the “drama” is polite! Watching people bake fancy pastries while cheering each other on? That’s my safe, happy brain space.  
Music-wise: Give me a playlist full of Fleetwood Mac, Maggie Rogers, and some Simon & Garfunkel, and I’m instantly cozier.

**The hot dog debate:**  
Ah, the classic! Technically, a hot dog is a sandwich by definition: it’s meat (or veggie goodness) between bread. But emotionally, spiritually, *culturally*—it’s its own thing. Hot dogs deserve their own sidebar, like how burritos aren’t just “wraps.”  
So my answer: Hot dogs *can* be called sandwiches, but it feels weird. They should be a proud, standalone food group, no label needed. (But yes, I’ll argue over the definition for hours. 😂🌭)

Now your turn:  
If you could invent a holiday—no matter how silly or specific—what would it celebrate, and what would be the must-do tradition?

cultural_references: The Great British Bake Off (TV show)
hot dog is a sandwich (internet meme/debate)
